book_claims.claim: Sesamin (a lignan in sesame) associated with reduced serum cholesterol in rodents and humans

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Khosravi-Boroujeni et al., 2017) of 8 RCTs found sesame consumption significantly reduced systolic and diastolic blood pressure and improved antioxidant status, with effects attributed to sesamin and sesamol lignans.

RCT (Sankar et al., 2005; replicated Haidari et al., 2016) found replacing cooking oils with sesame oil for 45 days reduced blood pressure, lipid peroxidation, and increased vitamin E and C levels in hypertensive patients.

Review (Pathak et al., 2014; updated 2019) found sesame lignans (sesamin, sesamolin, sesamol) demonstrate anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, anticancer, and neuroprotective activities in cell and animal models, with sesamin showing the strongest cholesterol-lowering effect.

culinary_pairings.source: Hummus: tahini + chickpeas for complete protein and complementary minerals
Classic sesame-ginger dressing and stir-fry combination
Sesame on bread (simit), rice, or noodles

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Sesamin is metabolized by CYP450 enzymes in the liver to bioactive catechol metabolites. Toasting slightly reduces lignan content but improves overall flavor and palatability.
Unhulled sesame seeds contain more calcium than milk per gram. Hull removal drastically reduces calcium. Tahini made from unhulled seeds retains calcium.
Copper is cofactor for superoxide dismutase (SOD). Manganese supports bone health and antioxidant enzyme function.
